settings.title: Karissa Sparke
settings.description:
Somewhere along the way, many of us find ourselves in the pursuit of having it all. A quiet balancing act between purpose, fulfilment and the longing for stillness. This sentiment sits at the heart of the Saira collection. A reflection on intuition, balance and the gentle unfolding of life’s seasons. For where intuition leads, balance follows.

It’s a philosophy that resonates deeply with Karissa Sparke, whose path has been shaped less by certainty and more by learning to trust the subtle signals within.

“I’ve never really resonated with the word intuition,” she shares. “But over the past year I’ve started noticing something more physical - like a clear yes or no in my body.”

Rather than waiting for a perfect plan, Karissa has begun to follow this feeling, even when it doesn’t fully make sense yet. Some of her most meaningful decisions have come from trusting that inner response, and allowing instinct to lead.

It’s a quiet shift, one that mirrors the spirit of Saira. A reminder that clarity often emerges not through control, but through trust.

settings.content_intro:
DO: BUY WHAT YOU LOVE

Natalie: Simply, a piece of vintage jewellery should be chosen because it resonates with you, and because you fall in love with it. Not only are they imbued with the histories and stories of their past owners, but vintage jewellery also provides an opportunity to embrace sustainable purchasing habits – offering once-cherished designs a second (or third) lease on life.

I have always had a love for vintage jewellery, or vintage anything really. I used to spend hours in op shops collecting all sorts of odd finds – furniture, clothes, hats, even once a vintage suitcase which I used to transport and display my first ever collection! I love the mystery and enigmatic nature of vintage pieces, the opportunity for speculation on their history and journey. I also have a deep reverence for the incredible craftsmanship many of these piece’s hold. So much tradition and heritage exists in every tiny detail, which is where my love for vintage jewellery originates from.

I encourage you to find your spark. Is it a particular stone colour or cut, the setting or design style or maybe even the piece’s provenance and era that speaks to you? Is it because it reminds you of a place, someone special or loved ones from generations past? I always like to remind clients that jewellery doesn’t have to be engraved or marked with a special stone to become solely yours – it can be anything about the piece that sparks a feeling or takes you back to a moment in time.

There’s a sense of romance and allure inherit in vintage pieces, and finding the perfect design should feel akin to falling in love: a lightning bolt of recognition; the sense that you two were destined to be connected.

settings.description: For Helena Vestergaard, intuition has been the quiet thread guiding her path. From trusting the risks that felt right, to learning through experience, her life today is shaped by listening inwardly. In the midst of motherhood and creative independence, she continues to navigate the delicate balance between being fully present for her children and honouring her own sense of self.
settings.description_mobile: For Helena Vestergaard, intuition has been the quiet thread guiding her path. From trusting the risks that felt right, to learning through experience, her life today is shaped by listening inwardly. In the midst of motherhood and creative independence, she continues to navigate the delicate balance between being fully present for her children and honouring her own sense of self.

settings.subheading: How has your instinct/inner guidance shaped the path you find yourself on today?
settings.description:
"I’ve never really resonated with the word intuition, but over the past year I’ve started noticing something a bit more physical - like a kind of clear yes or no in my body.

Some of the bigger decisions I’ve made lately didn’t come from having a full plan, they came from following that feeling, even when it didn’t fully make sense yet. I think that’s shaped my path more than anything - just paying attention to what feels right, and being willing to trust it before I can explain it, and even if it might not make sense to anybody else."
settings.description_mobile:
"I’ve never really resonated with the word intuition, but over the past year I’ve started noticing something a bit more physical - like a kind of clear yes or no in my body.

Some of the bigger decisions I’ve made lately didn’t come from having a full plan, they came from following that feeling, even when it didn’t fully make sense yet. I think that’s shaped my path more than anything - just paying attention to what feels right, and being willing to trust it before I can explain it, and even if it might not make sense to anybody else."

settings.description:
In this season of life, stillness doesn’t come from structured rituals, but from the rhythm of everyday moments.

Living on an island, surrounded by ocean and nature, has created space for slower, more intuitive living. The pace moves differently, softer, less defined, and it’s here that Karissa finds herself returning to presence.

“Surfing involves a lot of waiting - just sitting, watching, being present with what’s in front of you.”

These quiet pauses in the water have become her grounding practice. A place to reconnect with gratitude, settle her thoughts, and return to herself.

It’s in these in-between moments that balance begins to emerge naturally. Not forced, but found.
settings.description_mobile:

Living on an island, surrounded by ocean and nature, has created space for slower, more intuitive living. The pace moves differently, softer, less defined, and it’s here that Karissa finds herself returning to presence.

“Surfing involves a lot of waiting - just sitting, watching, being present with what’s in front of you.”

These quiet pauses in the water have become her grounding practice. A place to reconnect with gratitude, settle her thoughts, and return to herself.

It’s in these in-between moments that balance begins to emerge naturally. Not forced, but found.

settings.subheading: How do you create moments of stillness in your everyday life - a practice that helps you return to yourself?
settings.description:
"I don’t have a strict practice around stillness at the moment, I’ve had a strong meditation practice in the past and have experience such value in it. But am still finding my way back to it in this season of life. But I’ve found I’m experiencing these moments quite naturally through the ocean and the nature of living on a island where the pace moves a little slower… and not to strict schedule.

Surfing involves a lot of waiting - just sitting, watching, being present with what’s in front of you.

That’s probably where I feel most grounded… a way to come back to myself, to presence, gratitude and joy in my everyday life, especially when my mind feels turbulent."

That’s probably where I feel most grounded… a way to come back to myself, to presence, gratitude and joy in my everyday life, especially when my mind feels turbulent."

settings.subheading: As a woman, what opposing qualities is life asking you to balance right now? And which of those do you want to honour and make space for moving forward?
settings.description:
"Right now it feels like I’m balancing uncertainty with a desire for stability.

I used to be someone who tried to control everything - even small changes to routine could feel unsettling.

But over time I’ve started to see how adaptable we actually are.

There’s a kind of freedom in not knowing exactly how things will unfold, even if that still feels uncomfortable at times.

I’m learning to trust that things will work out in their own way - not always how I expected, but often in ways I couldn’t have planned.

And to stay open to that, rather than trying to control it."

settings.description:
Like the Saira collection, Karissa’s journey reflects the quiet balance between movement and stillness, certainty and openness, intention and surrender.

A reminder that we don’t need to have everything figured out.

Sometimes, the most meaningful paths begin by simply trusting what feels right and allowing balance to follow.

settings.title: Saira
settings.description:
Somewhere along the way, we have found ourselves in the pursuit of having it all - an endless balancing act of family, purpose and fulfilment. Each pulls us in different directions, while beneath it all, we long for stillness.

The repeating floral motif, with no beginning or end, evokes the infinite rhythm of our experience - a reminder that life is not linear, but cyclical. Every turn invites trust. Every choice offers a return to ourselves.

Saira marks the journey that begins within. A return to self. A step forward in trust. For where intuition leads, balance follows.
